visa_types.name: Employment
visa_types.documents_required:
For the documents required checklist please click here (http://www.bmbah.hu/)
List of approved insurance companies 
applicants about important information 
Please Note:
Please Note:
The processing of residence permit applications can take up to 8 weeks, in certain cases even longer.​
The decision on residence permit applications falls within the competence of the National Directorate-General for Aliens Policing of Hungary. The applicant will be notified by the Consular Section of the Embassy of Hungary in Nairobi as soon as it receives the decision. The Embassy cannot and will not reply to enquiries in relation to the status of applications.​
Original documents (e.g. birth certificates, diplomas) shall be presented to the Consul at the visa interview. Copies must be submitted with the application.​
The authorities reserve the right to examine the authenticity of the presented data and documents. Presentation of false data will lead to refusal.
In case of successful application, the applicant will get a visa allowing single entry to Hungary to collect the residence permit. The visa will be valid for 30 days, within the three months following the positive decision. Applicants are advised to enter Hungary at least 3–4 weeks before the end of the visa validity.
General Requirements:
- Applicant's passport + copy of the data page
- Passport issued within last 10 years, minimum 2 blank pages
- Completed and signed application form with relevant appendix
- One passport-size colour photo (not older than 6 months)
- Supporting documents regarding purpose of stay
- Proof of accommodation in Hungary
- Documents certifying sufficient means of subsistence
- Health insurance or financial equivalent
- Visa fee of KES 15,498 (by card or MPESA at Embassy)
Residence Permit for the Purpose of Employment:
- Work permit
- Contract of employment
- Certificate of qualifications
- Declaration on (expected) yearly income
- Proof of means of subsistence (bank statements, income certificates)
- Proof of accommodation (lease, ownership, boarding, reservation)
- Health insurance documents
- Conditions of exit (valid passport and return ticket/funds)
Residence Permit for the Purpose of Studies:
- Sworn affidavit of support from parent/guardian
- Proof of sufficient means (bank statements, employer letter)
- Evidence of relationship with sponsor (birth certificates)
- Original and copy of University Admission Letter
- Proof of registration
- Evidence of tuition and accommodation fee payments
- Previous academic records (original and copies)
- Evidence of booked accommodation
Residence Permit for Family Reunification:
- Copy of relative's passport
- Proof of family relationship (birth/marriage/adoption certificates)
- Proof of accommodation (lease, ownership, invitation letter, courtesy)
- Proof of means of subsistence (bank statements, tax documents, employment certificates)
Appeal Procedure:
If the visa is refused, applicants may appeal within 8 calendar days at the Hungarian Consulate. Appeals must be submitted in writing and signed by the applicant or power of attorney holder.
- Appeal fee: KES 22,543 (non-refundable)
- Appeal must be in Hungarian or any official EU language (with translation if not)
- Supporting documents can be submitted during the appeal
- The applicant may be called for a visa interview
- Appeal judgment may be subject to judicial review
A new application may be submitted at any time, but visa fee must be paid again.
Records of Data & Data Protection:
As per Article 96(1) of Act II of 2007, the immigration authority shall process the following data:
- Natural identification data
- Facial photograph
- Travel document details
- Purpose and duration of stay, previous residence
- Supporting document details
- Reasons for refusal or withdrawal of permit
- Permit number, serial, and validity
- Date of entry/exit, next country of residence
- Accommodation address
- Facial image and fingerprint images per EU regulations
Data may be shared with law enforcement, immigration, health, employment, and municipal agencies, and Schengen/EU consulates.
Applicants may request access to their data or correction of incorrect records, unless restricted by law.
Photograph Requirements [1]:
- Not older than 6 months
- Colour
- Passport-size (35–40 mm width)
- Sharp, high quality, full face
- Plain, light background
- Shawl only for religious purposes
- Face must be clearly visible
- Glasses allowed only if eyes are visible and no reflection
If the photo does not meet the above requirements, the applicant must provide a new photo or have one taken at the consulate.

Logistic Fees
Service Charge for Visa Application – 1300
Please Note:
The applicable visa fee in KES is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
visa_types.processing_time:
According to the Visa Code, the procedure time for processing Schengen visa D applications is up to 8 weeks. However, this is at the discretion of the consulate.
Kindly note that you will also be required to attend a face-to-face interview at the Consulate on a date that will be communicated to you after submitting your application.
Please note that long term visa applications are only accepted from Monday-Wednesday.
visa_types.forms:
Forms listed below must be filled in completely (in English only) and must be signed by the applicant himself/herself.
Please note that application form for Schengen visa should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be accepted.
Visa application form can be downloaded and printed from the link below. Forms are also available at the visa application centre (free of charge).
Schengen Visa Application Form 

 Appendix Employment
Seasonal employment form
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Applicant going for Employment purpose to apply for the given category.
Applicants may apply for seasonal employment visas only if:
- The employment duration does not exceed 180 days within a 12-month period, and
- The employment is strictly within the agricultural sector
*The Hungarian immigration authorities have issued a new directive, effective from 6 June 2026, under which residence permit applications submitted for employment purposes including seasonal employment will no longer be approved.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Applicant going for Employment purpose to apply for the given category.
Applicants may apply for seasonal employment visas only if:
- The employment duration does not exceed 180 days within a 12-month period, and
- The employment is strictly within the agricultural sector
*The Hungarian immigration authorities have issued a new directive, effective from 6 June 2026, under which residence permit applications submitted for employment purposes including seasonal employment will no longer be approved.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Employment]
Logistic Fees
Service Charge for Visa Application – 1300
Please Note:
The applicable visa fee in KES is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.

## Documents Required
For the documents required checklist please click here (http://www.bmbah.hu/)

Please Note:
Please Note:
The processing of residence permit applications can take up to 8 weeks, in certain cases even longer.​
The decision on residence permit applications falls within the competence of the National Directorate-General for Aliens Policing of Hungary. The applicant will be notified by the Consular Section of the Embassy of Hungary in Nairobi as soon as it receives the decision. The Embassy cannot and will not reply to enquiries in relation to the status of applications.​
Original documents (e.g. birth certificates, diplomas) shall be presented to the Consul at the visa interview. Copies must be submitted with the application.​
The authorities reserve the right to examine the authenticity of the presented data and documents. Presentation of false data will lead to refusal.
In case of successful application, the applicant will get a visa allowing single entry to Hungary to collect the residence permit. The visa will be valid for 30 days, within the three months following the positive decision. Applicants are advised to enter Hungary at least 3–4 weeks before the end of the visa validity.
General Requirements:
- Applicant's passport + copy of the data page
- Passport issued within last 10 years, minimum 2 blank pages
- Completed and signed application form with relevant appendix
- One passport-size colour photo (not older than 6 months)
- Supporting documents regarding purpose of stay
- Proof of accommodation in Hungary
- Documents certifying sufficient means of subsistence
- Health insurance or financial equivalent
- Visa fee of KES 15,498 (by card or MPESA at Embassy)
Residence Permit for the Purpose of Employment:
- Work permit
- Contract of employment
- Certificate of qualifications
- Declaration on (expected) yearly income
- Proof of means of subsistence (bank statements, income certificates)
- Proof of accommodation (lease, ownership, boarding, reservation)
- Health insurance documents
- Conditions of exit (valid passport and return ticket/funds)
Residence Permit for the Purpose of Studies:
- Sworn affidavit of support from parent/guardian
- Proof of sufficient means (bank statements, employer letter)
- Evidence of relationship with sponsor (birth certificates)
- Original and copy of University Admission Letter
- Proof of registration
- Evidence of tuition and accommodation fee payments
- Previous academic records (original and copies)
- Evidence of booked accommodation
Residence Permit for Family Reunification:
- Copy of relative's passport
- Proof of family relationship (birth/marriage/adoption certificates)
- Proof of accommodation (lease, ownership, invitation letter, courtesy)
- Proof of means of subsistence (bank statements, tax documents, employment certificates)
Appeal Procedure:
If the visa is refused, applicants may appeal within 8 calendar days at the Hungarian Consulate. Appeals must be submitted in writing and signed by the applicant or power of attorney holder.
- Appeal fee: KES 22,543 (non-refundable)
- Appeal must be in Hungarian or any official EU language (with translation if not)
- Supporting documents can be submitted during the appeal
- The applicant may be called for a visa interview
- Appeal judgment may be subject to judicial review
A new application may be submitted at any time, but visa fee must be paid again.
Records of Data & Data Protection:
As per Article 96(1) of Act II of 2007, the immigration authority shall process the following data:
- Natural identification data
- Facial photograph
- Travel document details
- Purpose and duration of stay, previous residence
- Supporting document details
- Reasons for refusal or withdrawal of permit
- Permit number, serial, and validity
- Date of entry/exit, next country of residence
- Accommodation address
- Facial image and fingerprint images per EU regulations
Data may be shared with law enforcement, immigration, health, employment, and municipal agencies, and Schengen/EU consulates.
Applicants may request access to their data or correction of incorrect records, unless restricted by law.
Photograph Requirements [1]:
- Not older than 6 months
- Colour
- Passport-size (35–40 mm width)
- Sharp, high quality, full face
- Plain, light background
- Shawl only for religious purposes
- Face must be clearly visible
- Glasses allowed only if eyes are visible and no reflection
If the photo does not meet the above requirements, the applicant must provide a new photo or have one taken at the consulate.

## Photo Specifications
All photos must meet the requirements listed below. If these requirements have not been met, the applicant will have to deliver a new photo that meets the requirements. The photo requirements are the same for children as for adults.
The Photo shall:
- be in sharp focus, clear and with good contrasts
- be printed on quality photo paper with high solution
- should be taken against a White background
- be 35x45 mm in width
- maximum 6 months old, resemble the applicant
- full-face, facing the camera directly, eyes looking straight at the camera
- show the whole head and the top of the shoulders. The face shall occupy 70-80 % of the picture
- show open eyes that are clearly visible. Hair shall not cover the eyes
Regarding glasses: 
Should be without glasses
Regarding head-dress:
Religious head-dresses are allowed provided that all details of the face are clearly shown.
- Chin, forehead and both cheeks shall all be clearly visible
- Shadows in the face due to the head-dress are not allowed

## Processing Time
According to the Visa Code, the procedure time for processing Schengen visa D applications is up to 8 weeks. However, this is at the discretion of the consulate.
Kindly note that you will also be required to attend a face-to-face interview at the Consulate on a date that will be communicated to you after submitting your application.
Please note that long term visa applications are only accepted from Monday-Wednesday.

## Download Forms
Forms listed below must be filled in completely (in English only) and must be signed by the applicant himself/herself.
Please note that application form for Schengen visa should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be accepted.
Visa application form can be downloaded and printed from the link below. Forms are also available at the visa application centre (free of charge).
Schengen Visa Application Form 

 Appendix Employment
Seasonal employment form

visa_types.name: Tourism visa
visa_types.documents_required:
- Travel Medical Insurance is compulsory for Schengen visas under Regulation (EU) 2019/1155 of the European Parliament. If you haven’t purchased yet, please click here (https://cps.ssish.com/axa/astp/home?utm_source=vfsglobal_mission_site&utm_medium=website&utm_campaign=Hungary_China)
- Click on the link below for documents required checklist.
Individual Schengen-visa Checklist
List of approved insurance companies 
applicants about important information 
Important Notice
Please note: A visa is a preliminary permission for entry, which does not automatically entitle its bearer to enter the Schengen area. The traveller may be requested at the border to demonstrate that the conditions for his/her entry and stay in Hungary are met. If the conditions under which the visa had been issued are no longer met, the traveller may be denied entry.
We only accept visa applications submitted at least 15 calendar days and maximum 6 months before the date of intended departure*. It is recommended to submit the application earlier than 15 days before the planned departure date, as the visa processing time may be extended up to 45 days. The consulate does not take any responsibility for delayed submission and for therefore failed travels. Please take this into account when booking your appointment.
Requirements
- Application form - completed and signed*
- Travel document* valid for travel to the destination Member States
- Copy of the data page of the passport, any visas, residence permits, entry and exit stamps
- Copy of the national ID card (both sides)
- One colour photo*, max six months old, passport size (45 mm x 35 mm)
- Payment of the visa fee* (KES 12,680 from 11th June 2024)
- Providing biometric data (digital photo and fingerprints)*
- Individual medical travel insurance (min EUR 30,000 coverage)
- For non-Kenyan residents: Copy of valid Kenyan residence permit
- Proof of accommodation and flight ticket booking
- Proof of sufficient means of subsistence (bank statements, payslips, etc.)
If Employed:
- Signed and stamped letter from employer with personal and job details
If Self-employed:
- Copy of Kenya PIN Certificate and certified personal bank statement
If Retired:
- Documents proving pension or financial support
If Student or Pupil:
- Letter from institution confirming break/absence during travel
Additional Requirements
Tourism:
- Detailed travel itinerary
- Documents relating to personal ties in Kenya
Visits (Family & Friends):
- Letter of invitation with relationship details
- Proof of accommodation if provided by inviting person
- Copy of ID/passport of inviting person
- Any other proof of relationship or personal ties
Business & Conference:
- Signed/stamped letter of invitation
- Letter from employer detailing purpose and leave
- Pay slips and bank statements (last 3 months)
- Conference registration and payment proof (if applicable)
Minors (under 18 years):
- Both parents present at interview (or justification documents)
- Birth certificate copy
- Parents' ID copies
- Certified bank statements of sponsor(s)
- Notarized consent letter from both parents
- Custody documents (if guardian is not biological parent)
- Letter from school with student details and holidays
- List of group members (if travelling in group)
Family Members of EEA Citizens
- Simplified visa application (free of charge)
- Proof of family relationship
- Proof of EEA citizenship
- Proof of joint travel with EEA citizen
Note: Does not apply to family members of Hungarian citizens.
Decision Making
- Schengen system and authority consultation
- May require additional documents or interviews
Refusal
Applications will be refused if:
- False or forged documents
- Lack of travel purpose or means
- Travel ban/SIS alert
- Threat to public/security/health
- Lack of valid travel medical insurance
Refusal reasons are provided in person. Visa fees are non-refundable.
Appeal Procedure
- Appeals within 8 calendar days of refusal (3 days for annulment)
- Written, signed appeal required
- Appeal fee: KES 22,543 (non-refundable)
- Additional documents and interviews may be required
A new application may be submitted after the appeal deadline (8 days) has passed. New fee applies.
Important: If your application fails to fulfil any of the above requirements marked with an asterisk (*), it is considered inadmissible and your documents, including application fee, will be returned without notice. (Visa Codex art. 19:3)
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:  Tourism visa]
The visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following categories:
- children under six years;
- school pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training;
- researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in Recommendation No 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research;
- representatives of non-profit organizations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
The visa fee may be waived for:
- children from the age of six years and below the age of 12 years.
- holders of diplomatic and service passports; (in case of Hungary the visa fee only for the holders of diplomatic passports can be waived)
- Participants aged 25 years or less in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events, organized by nonprofit organizations.
Within local Schengen cooperation, Members States shall aim to harmonize the application of these exemptions.
Logistic Fees
Service Charge for Visa Application – KES 1300

Please Note:
The applicable visa fee in KES is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.

The consulate does not take any responsibility for delayed submission and for therefore failed travels. Please take this into account when booking your appointment.

Please download, print, fill, sign and submit the consent form along with your visa application form, without which we will not be able to proceed with the application lodgement. Click here
visa_types.overview:
Overview
It is applicable in case you are travelling for tourism. This visa is given for a period of the stay up to 90 days within 180 days.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
It is applicable in case you are travelling for tourism. This visa is given for a period of the stay up to 90 days within 180 days.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Tourism visa]
The visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following categories:
- children under six years;
- school pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training;
- researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in Recommendation No 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research;
- representatives of non-profit organizations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
The visa fee may be waived for:
- children from the age of six years and below the age of 12 years.
- holders of diplomatic and service passports; (in case of Hungary the visa fee only for the holders of diplomatic passports can be waived)
- Participants aged 25 years or less in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events, organized by nonprofit organizations.
Within local Schengen cooperation, Members States shall aim to harmonize the application of these exemptions.
Logistic Fees
Service Charge for Visa Application – KES 1300

Please Note:
The applicable visa fee in KES is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.

## Documents Required
- Travel Medical Insurance is compulsory for Schengen visas under Regulation (EU) 2019/1155 of the European Parliament. If you haven’t purchased yet, please click here (https://cps.ssish.com/axa/astp/home?utm_source=vfsglobal_mission_site&utm_medium=website&utm_campaign=Hungary_China)
- Click on the link below for documents required checklist.
Individual Schengen-visa Checklist
List of approved insurance companies 
applicants about important information 
Important Notice
Please note: A visa is a preliminary permission for entry, which does not automatically entitle its bearer to enter the Schengen area. The traveller may be requested at the border to demonstrate that the conditions for his/her entry and stay in Hungary are met. If the conditions under which the visa had been issued are no longer met, the traveller may be denied entry.
We only accept visa applications submitted at least 15 calendar days and maximum 6 months before the date of intended departure*. It is recommended to submit the application earlier than 15 days before the planned departure date, as the visa processing time may be extended up to 45 days. The consulate does not take any responsibility for delayed submission and for therefore failed travels. Please take this into account when booking your appointment.
Requirements
- Application form - completed and signed*
- Travel document* valid for travel to the destination Member States
- Copy of the data page of the passport, any visas, residence permits, entry and exit stamps
- Copy of the national ID card (both sides)
- One colour photo*, max six months old, passport size (45 mm x 35 mm)
- Payment of the visa fee* (KES 12,680 from 11th June 2024)
- Providing biometric data (digital photo and fingerprints)*
- Individual medical travel insurance (min EUR 30,000 coverage)
- For non-Kenyan residents: Copy of valid Kenyan residence permit
- Proof of accommodation and flight ticket booking
- Proof of sufficient means of subsistence (bank statements, payslips, etc.)
If Employed:
- Signed and stamped letter from employer with personal and job details
If Self-employed:
- Copy of Kenya PIN Certificate and certified personal bank statement
If Retired:
- Documents proving pension or financial support
If Student or Pupil:
- Letter from institution confirming break/absence during travel
Additional Requirements
Tourism:
- Detailed travel itinerary
- Documents relating to personal ties in Kenya
Visits (Family & Friends):
- Letter of invitation with relationship details
- Proof of accommodation if provided by inviting person
- Copy of ID/passport of inviting person
- Any other proof of relationship or personal ties
Business & Conference:
- Signed/stamped letter of invitation
- Letter from employer detailing purpose and leave
- Pay slips and bank statements (last 3 months)
- Conference registration and payment proof (if applicable)
Minors (under 18 years):
- Both parents present at interview (or justification documents)
- Birth certificate copy
- Parents' ID copies
- Certified bank statements of sponsor(s)
- Notarized consent letter from both parents
- Custody documents (if guardian is not biological parent)
- Letter from school with student details and holidays
- List of group members (if travelling in group)
Family Members of EEA Citizens
- Simplified visa application (free of charge)
- Proof of family relationship
- Proof of EEA citizenship
- Proof of joint travel with EEA citizen
Note: Does not apply to family members of Hungarian citizens.
Decision Making
- Schengen system and authority consultation
- May require additional documents or interviews
Refusal
Applications will be refused if:
- False or forged documents
- Lack of travel purpose or means
- Travel ban/SIS alert
- Threat to public/security/health
- Lack of valid travel medical insurance
Refusal reasons are provided in person. Visa fees are non-refundable.
Appeal Procedure
- Appeals within 8 calendar days of refusal (3 days for annulment)
- Written, signed appeal required
- Appeal fee: KES 22,543 (non-refundable)
- Additional documents and interviews may be required
A new application may be submitted after the appeal deadline (8 days) has passed. New fee applies.
Important: If your application fails to fulfil any of the above requirements marked with an asterisk (*), it is considered inadmissible and your documents, including application fee, will be returned without notice. (Visa Codex art. 19:3)

## Photo Specifications
All photos must meet the requirements listed below. If these requirements have not been met, the applicant will have to deliver a new photo that meets the requirements. The photo requirements are the same for children as for adults.
The Photo shall:
- be in sharp focus, clear and with good contrasts
- be printed on quality photo paper with high solution
- should be taken against a White background
- be 35x45 mm in width
- maximum 6 months old, resemble the applicant
- full-face, facing the camera directly, eyes looking straight at the camera
- show the whole head and the top of the shoulders. The face shall occupy 70-80 % of the picture
- show open eyes that are clearly visible. Hair shall not cover the eyes
Regarding glasses: 
Should be without glasses
Regarding head-dress:
Religious head-dresses are allowed provided that all details of the face are clearly shown.
- Chin, forehead and both cheeks shall all be clearly visible
- Shadows in the face due to the head-dress are not allowed

## Processing Time
We only accept visa applications submitted at least 15 calendar days and maximum 6 months before the date of intended departure. It is recommended to submit the application earlier than 15 days before the planned departure date, as the visa processing time may be extended up to 45 days. The consulate does not take any responsibility for delayed submission and for therefore failed travels. Please take this into account when booking your appointment.

## Download Forms
Forms listed below must be filled in completely (in English only) and must be signed by the applicant himself/herself.
Please note that application form for Schengen visa should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be accepted.
Visa application form can be downloaded and printed from the link below. Forms are also available at the visa application centre (free of charge).
Schengen Visa Application Form 
According to the Personal Information Protection Law of the People’s Republic of China and other applicable laws and regulations to protect your personal information, VFS Global must obtain the consent from you for the processing of your personal data and proceed with your visa application. Please download, print, fill, sign and submit the consent form along with your visa application form, without which we will not be able to proceed with the application lodgement. Click here

visa_types.name: Family reunification
visa_types.documents_required:
For the documents required checklist please click here (http://www.bmbah.hu/)
List of approved insurance companies 
applicants about important information 
Please Note:
The processing of residence permit applications can take up to 8 weeks, in certain cases even longer.​
The decision on residence permit applications falls within the competence of the National Directorate-General for Aliens Policing of Hungary. The applicant will be notified by the Consular Section of the Embassy of Hungary in Nairobi as soon as it receives the decision. The Embassy cannot and will not reply to enquiries in relation to the status of applications.​
Original documents (e.g. birth certificates, diplomas) shall be presented to the Consul at the visa interview. Copies must be submitted with the application.​
The authorities reserve the right to examine the authenticity of the presented data and documents. Presentation of false data will lead to refusal.
In case of successful application, the applicant will get a visa allowing single entry to Hungary to collect the residence permit. The visa will be valid for 30 days, within the three months following the positive decision. Applicants are advised to enter Hungary at least 3–4 weeks before the end of the visa validity.
General Requirements:
- Applicant's passport + copy of the data page
- Passport issued within last 10 years, minimum 2 blank pages
- Completed and signed application form with relevant appendix
- One passport-size colour photo (not older than 6 months)
- Supporting documents regarding purpose of stay
- Proof of accommodation in Hungary
- Documents certifying sufficient means of subsistence
- Health insurance or financial equivalent
- Visa fee of KES 15,498 (by card or MPESA at Embassy)
Residence Permit for the Purpose of Employment:
- Work permit
- Contract of employment
- Certificate of qualifications
- Declaration on (expected) yearly income
- Proof of means of subsistence (bank statements, income certificates)
- Proof of accommodation (lease, ownership, boarding, reservation)
- Health insurance documents
- Conditions of exit (valid passport and return ticket/funds)
Residence Permit for the Purpose of Studies:
- Sworn affidavit of support from parent/guardian
- Proof of sufficient means (bank statements, employer letter)
- Evidence of relationship with sponsor (birth certificates)
- Original and copy of University Admission Letter
- Proof of registration
- Evidence of tuition and accommodation fee payments
- Previous academic records (original and copies)
- Evidence of booked accommodation
Residence Permit for Family Reunification:
- Copy of relative's passport
- Proof of family relationship (birth/marriage/adoption certificates)
- Proof of accommodation (lease, ownership, invitation letter, courtesy)
- Proof of means of subsistence (bank statements, tax documents, employment certificates)
Appeal Procedure:
If the visa is refused, applicants may appeal within 8 calendar days at the Hungarian Consulate. Appeals must be submitted in writing and signed by the applicant or power of attorney holder.
- Appeal fee: KES 22,543 (non-refundable)
- Appeal must be in Hungarian or any official EU language (with translation if not)
- Supporting documents can be submitted during the appeal
- The applicant may be called for a visa interview
- Appeal judgment may be subject to judicial review
A new application may be submitted at any time, but visa fee must be paid again.
Records of Data & Data Protection:
As per Article 96(1) of Act II of 2007, the immigration authority shall process the following data:
- Natural identification data
- Facial photograph
- Travel document details
- Purpose and duration of stay, previous residence
- Supporting document details
- Reasons for refusal or withdrawal of permit
- Permit number, serial, and validity
- Date of entry/exit, next country of residence
- Accommodation address
- Facial image and fingerprint images per EU regulations
Data may be shared with law enforcement, immigration, health, employment, and municipal agencies, and Schengen/EU consulates.
Applicants may request access to their data or correction of incorrect records, unless restricted by law.
Photograph Requirements [1]:
- Not older than 6 months
- Colour
- Passport-size (35–40 mm width)
- Sharp, high quality, full face
- Plain, light background
- Shawl only for religious purposes
- Face must be clearly visible
- Glasses allowed only if eyes are visible and no reflection
If the photo does not meet the above requirements, the applicant must provide a new photo or have one taken at the consulate.

[Table:  Family reunification]
Logistic Fees
Service Charge for Visa Application – 1300
Please Note:
The applicable visa fee in KES is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
visa_types.processing_time:
According to the Visa Code, the procedure time for processing Schengen visa D applications can take up to 8 weeks. However, this is at the discretion of the consulate.
Kindly note that you will also be required to attend a face-to-face interview at the Consulate on a date that will be communicated to you after submitting your application.
Please note that long term visa applications are only accepted from Monday-Wednesday.

- Plain, light background

If the photo does not meet the above requirements, the applicant must provide a new photo or have one taken at the consulate.

The Photo shall:
- be in sharp focus, clear and with good contrasts
- be printed on quality photo paper with high solution
- should be taken against a White background
- be 35x45 mm in width
- maximum 6 months old, resemble the applicant
- full-face, facing the camera directly, eyes looking straight at the camera
- show the whole head and the top of the shoulders. The face shall occupy 70-80 % of the picture
- show open eyes that are clearly visible. Hair shall not cover the eyes
Regarding glasses: 
Should be without glasses
Regarding head-dress:
Religious head-dresses are allowed provided that all details of the face are clearly shown.
- Chin, forehead and both cheeks shall all be clearly visible
- Shadows in the face due to the head-dress are not allowed

## Processing Time
According to the Visa Code, the procedure time for processing Schengen visa D applications can take up to 8 weeks. However, this is at the discretion of the consulate.
Kindly note that you will also be required to attend a face-to-face interview at the Consulate on a date that will be communicated to you after submitting your application.
Please note that long term visa applications are only accepted from Monday-Wednesday.

- Payment of the visa fee* (KES 12,680 from 11th June 2024)
- Providing biometric data (digital photo and fingerprints)*
- Individual medical travel insurance (min EUR 30,000 coverage)
- For non-Kenyan residents: Copy of valid Kenyan residence permit
- Proof of accommodation and flight ticket booking
- Proof of sufficient means of subsistence (bank statements, payslips, etc.)
If Employed:
- Signed and stamped letter from employer with personal and job details
If Self-employed:
- Copy of Kenya PIN Certificate and certified personal bank statement
If Retired:
- Documents proving pension or financial support
If Student or Pupil:
- Letter from institution confirming break/absence during travel
Additional Requirements
Tourism:
- Detailed travel itinerary
- Documents relating to personal ties in Kenya
Visits (Family & Friends):
- Letter of invitation with relationship details
- Proof of accommodation if provided by inviting person
- Copy of ID/passport of inviting person
- Any other proof of relationship or personal ties
Business & Conference:
- Signed/stamped letter of invitation
- Letter from employer detailing purpose and leave
- Pay slips and bank statements (last 3 months)
- Conference registration and payment proof (if applicable)
Minors (under 18 years):
- Both parents present at interview (or justification documents)
- Birth certificate copy
- Parents' ID copies
- Certified bank statements of sponsor(s)
- Notarized consent letter from both parents
- Custody documents (if guardian is not biological parent)
- Letter from school with student details and holidays
- List of group members (if travelling in group)
Family Members of EEA Citizens
- Simplified visa application (free of charge)
- Proof of family relationship
- Proof of EEA citizenship
- Proof of joint travel with EEA citizen
Note: Does not apply to family members of Hungarian citizens.
Decision Making
- Schengen system and authority consultation
- May require additional documents or interviews
Refusal
Applications will be refused if:
- False or forged documents
- Lack of travel purpose or means
- Travel ban/SIS alert
- Threat to public/security/health
- Lack of valid travel medical insurance
Refusal reasons are provided in person. Visa fees are non-refundable.
Appeal Procedure
- Appeals within 8 calendar days of refusal (3 days for annulment)
- Written, signed appeal required
- Appeal fee: KES 22,543 (non-refundable)
- Additional documents and interviews may be required
A new application may be submitted after the appeal deadline (8 days) has passed. New fee applies.
Important: If your application fails to fulfil any of the above requirements marked with an asterisk (*), it is considered inadmissible and your documents, including application fee, will be returned without notice. (Visa Codex art. 19:3)

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Business visa application is applicable in case you wish to travel to Hungary for the purpose of business, conferences or other professional purposes please see the list of requirements for further details. This visa is given for travel period of less than 90 days.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Tourism visa]
Logistic Fees
Service Charge for Visa Application – 1300
Please Note:
The applicable visa fee in KES is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.

## Documents Required

- Travel Medical Insurance is compulsory for Schengen visas under Regulation (EU) 2019/1155 of the European Parliament. If you haven’t purchased yet, please click here (https://cps.ssish.com/axa/astp/home?utm_source=vfsglobal_mission_site&utm_medium=website&utm_campaign=Hungary_China)
- Click on the link below for documents required checklist.
Individual Schengen-visa Checklist
List of approved insurance companies 
applicants about important information 
Important Notice
Please note: A visa is a preliminary permission for entry, which does not automatically entitle its bearer to enter the Schengen area. The traveller may be requested at the border to demonstrate that the conditions for his/her entry and stay in Hungary are met. If the conditions under which the visa had been issued are no longer met, the traveller may be denied entry.
We only accept visa applications submitted at least 15 calendar days and maximum 6 months before the date of intended departure*. It is recommended to submit the application earlier than 15 days before the planned departure date, as the visa processing time may be extended up to 45 days. The consulate does not take any responsibility for delayed submission and for therefore failed travels. Please take this into account when booking your appointment.
Requirements
- Application form - completed and signed*
- Travel document* valid for travel to the destination Member States
- Copy of the data page of the passport, any visas, residence permits, entry and exit stamps
- Copy of the national ID card (both sides)
- One colour photo*, max six months old, passport size (45 mm x 35 mm)
- Payment of the visa fee* (KES 12,680 from 11th June 2024)
- Providing biometric data (digital photo and fingerprints)*
- Individual medical travel insurance (min EUR 30,000 coverage)
- For non-Kenyan residents: Copy of valid Kenyan residence permit
- Proof of accommodation and flight ticket booking
- Proof of sufficient means of subsistence (bank statements, payslips, etc.)
If Employed:
- Signed and stamped letter from employer with personal and job details
If Self-employed:
- Copy of Kenya PIN Certificate and certified personal bank statement
If Retired:
- Documents proving pension or financial support
If Student or Pupil:
- Letter from institution confirming break/absence during travel
Additional Requirements
Tourism:
- Detailed travel itinerary
- Documents relating to personal ties in Kenya
Visits (Family & Friends):
- Letter of invitation with relationship details
- Proof of accommodation if provided by inviting person
- Copy of ID/passport of inviting person
- Any other proof of relationship or personal ties
Business & Conference:
- Signed/stamped letter of invitation
- Letter from employer detailing purpose and leave
- Pay slips and bank statements (last 3 months)
- Conference registration and payment proof (if applicable)
Minors (under 18 years):
- Both parents present at interview (or justification documents)
- Birth certificate copy
- Parents' ID copies
- Certified bank statements of sponsor(s)
- Notarized consent letter from both parents
- Custody documents (if guardian is not biological parent)
- Letter from school with student details and holidays
- List of group members (if travelling in group)
Family Members of EEA Citizens
- Simplified visa application (free of charge)
- Proof of family relationship
- Proof of EEA citizenship
- Proof of joint travel with EEA citizen
Note: Does not apply to family members of Hungarian citizens.
Decision Making
- Schengen system and authority consultation
- May require additional documents or interviews
Refusal
Applications will be refused if:
- False or forged documents
- Lack of travel purpose or means
- Travel ban/SIS alert
- Threat to public/security/health
- Lack of valid travel medical insurance
Refusal reasons are provided in person. Visa fees are non-refundable.
Appeal Procedure
- Appeals within 8 calendar days of refusal (3 days for annulment)
- Written, signed appeal required
- Appeal fee: KES 22,543 (non-refundable)
- Additional documents and interviews may be required
A new application may be submitted after the appeal deadline (8 days) has passed. New fee applies.
Important: If your application fails to fulfil any of the above requirements marked with an asterisk (*), it is considered inadmissible and your documents, including application fee, will be returned without notice. (Visa Codex art. 19:3)

## Photo Specifications
All photos must meet the requirements listed below. If these requirements have not been met, the applicant will have to deliver a new photo that meets the requirements. The photo requirements are the same for children as for adults.
The Photo shall:
- be in sharp focus, clear and with good contrasts
- be printed on quality photo paper with high solution
- should be taken against a White background
- be 35x45 mm in width
- maximum 6 months old, resemble the applicant
- full-face, facing the camera directly, eyes looking straight at the camera
- show the whole head and the top of the shoulders. The face shall occupy 70-80 % of the picture
- show open eyes that are clearly visible. Hair shall not cover the eyes
Regarding glasses: 
Should be without glasses
Regarding head-dress:
Religious head-dresses are allowed provided that all details of the face are clearly shown.
- Chin, forehead and both cheeks shall all be clearly visible
- Shadows in the face due to the head-dress are not allowed

## Processing Time
We only accept visa applications submitted at least 15 calendar days and maximum 6 months before the date of intended departure. It is recommended to submit the application earlier than 15 days before the planned departure date, as the visa processing time may be extended up to 45 days. The consulate does not take any responsibility for delayed submission and for therefore failed travels. Please take this into account when booking your appointment.

## Download Forms
Forms listed below must be filled in completely (in English only) and must be signed by the applicant himself/herself.
Please note that application form for Schengen visa should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be accepted.
Visa application form can be downloaded and printed from the link below. Forms are also available at the visa application centre (free of charge).
Schengen Visa Application Form 
According to the Personal Information Protection Law of the People’s Republic of China and other applicable laws and regulations to protect your personal information, VFS Global must obtain the consent from you for the processing of your personal data and proceed with your visa application. Please download, print, fill, sign and submit the consent form along with your visa application form, without which we will not be able to proceed with the application lodgement. Click here
